name: The Emerald Enclave
headquarters: No fixed seat — members operate independently across the wilds
body:
## What You Know

The Emerald Enclave is not a kingdom or an army. It's a far-flung order of druids, rangers, and wilderness wanderers who share one purpose: protect the natural world and help civilization exist alongside it — not at its expense.

Enclave members operate in isolation from one another. You might encounter a lone ranger guiding a caravan through mountain passes, a druid appearing from nowhere to defend a besieged village, or a hermit healer who slips back into the forest before anyone learns their name. They don't march in formation. They don't hold councils in grand halls. They show up where they're needed, do what needs doing, and disappear.

What they oppose is clear: the unnatural. Undead. Elemental chaos. The forces of shadow that corrupt living land and twist the cycle of life and death. The Enclave has no love for the Spree's obsession with Shadow and undeath — they would view it as a direct assault on the natural order they've spent centuries protecting.

## What They Want
- Restore and preserve the natural order of the world
- Destroy all that is unnatural — undead, corrupted creatures, elemental chaos
- Keep the forces of civilization and wilderness in balance
- Protect sacred wild places and the creatures that depend on them

## Member Ranks
| Rank | Title |
|------|-------|
| 1 | Springwarden |
| 2 | Summerstrider |
| 3 | Autumnreaver |
| 4 | Winterstalker |
| 5 | Master of the Wild |

## How They View the Spree
The Enclave sees the Spree's obsession with Shadow and undeath as a fundamental violation of the natural cycle — life, death, rebirth — that they exist to protect. They would not negotiate with the Spree and would not remain neutral in a fight against them.

## Their View of the Party
TBD — depending on what relationship the party has established with Enclave members.

name: The Fey Alliance
insignia: The trinity knot — worn by all members as a symbol of unity
body:
## What You Know

The Fey Alliance isn't from here. For centuries they lived in Faerie — the Feywild — a realm of magic and natural harmony led by Oberon and Titania. Then something went wrong. A great magical disturbance collapsed the barriers between the Feywild and the prime material plane, and thousands of fey were suddenly thrust into a world they didn't choose.

Rather than scatter, they held together. Oberon and Titania established a hidden enclave in the High Forest, built relationships with other magical creatures, and adapted. When Silverymoon's wards fell during the Spree invasion and the city was left exposed, the Fey Alliance moved in. Oberon now governs the North Bank.

The Fey Alliance is not your enemy — but they're not straightforwardly your ally either. They are neutral in the war between the Lord's Alliance and the Spree, and they intend to stay that way. Their one real priority is getting home to the Feywild. Everything else is negotiable.

Fey are secretive by nature. They don't explain themselves to outsiders. What you see from the Fey Alliance is always the surface — what's underneath it is harder to read.

## What They Want
- Survive in the prime material plane
- **Get back to the Feywild** — this is their true north; everything else is in service of this goal
- Maintain the balance of nature in this realm while they're here
- Build enough alliances to protect themselves until they can go home

## Leadership
- **King Oberon** — The diplomat. Manages relationships between fey clans and with outside factions. The face of the Alliance.
- **Queen Titania** — The power. Sets the Alliance's true direction. More difficult to read than Oberon, more consequential to impress.

## Internal Ranks
| Rank | Role |
|------|------|
| King / Queen | Oberon and Titania |
| High Council | Senior advisors; clan representatives |
| Clan Leaders | Each fey clan's representative |
| Envoys | Ambassadors to outside groups |
| Guardians | Military defenders |
| Artisans | Crafters of magical items |

## How They View Other Factions
- **Lord's Alliance** — Complicated. They now hold Silverymoon, which was an Alliance city. There's no open hostility, but there's no warmth either.
- **Spree Alliance** — A serious threat to the natural order. The Spree's obsession with death and Shadow is the opposite of what the fey value.
- **The party** — TBD based on how you've interacted with them.

## What's Unclear
Why exactly were the fey displaced? What caused the magical disturbance that collapsed the Feywild barriers? The Fey Alliance doesn't say, and it's not clear they know.

name: The Harpers
headquarters: No fixed seat — agents work independently, in secret
body:
## What You Know

The Harpers don't advertise themselves. That's the point. They are a clandestine network of spies, spellcasters, and agents who work in the shadows to protect the weak, the poor, and the oppressed — and to stop any single power from growing so large it can dominate everyone else.

Harper agents rarely operate in the open. They cultivate identities, build information networks, and manipulate situations from behind the scenes. They know that knowledge is power, so they gather as much of it as they can. Veteran members have access to secret caches of intelligence and trusted contacts in every major city across Faerûn. When they must act, they act decisively. When they can disappear, they do.

The bond between Harpers is strong — their friendships border on unbreakable. But you'd rarely know two of them were connected just from watching them interact. That's by design.

## What They Want
- Gather intelligence throughout Faerûn
- Promote fairness and equality through covert means
- Thwart tyrants and any organization that grows too powerful
- Aid the weak, the poor, and the oppressed

## Member Ranks
| Rank | Title |
|------|-------|
| 1 | Watcher |
| 2 | Harpshadow |
| 3 | Brightcandle |
| 4 | Wise Owl |
| 5 | High Harper |

## How They View the Spree
The Harpers see the Spree as a serious threat to individual freedom — a group of dangerous extremists using religious ideology as cover for control and manipulation. The Spree's shadow network and intelligence operations are exactly the kind of power consolidation the Harpers exist to prevent.

## Their View of the Party
TBD — depending on what relationship the party has established with Harper agents.

name: Lord's Alliance
status: Weakening — losing ground across the North
insignia: Signet ring worn with the symbol on the palm side
body:
## What You Know

The Lords' Alliance is the coalition that has kept civilization alive in the North for generations. It's an association of rulers — the lords of Waterdeep, Silverymoon, Yartar, and other free cities — who agreed that united they stand, divided they fall. Alliance agents are some of the most skilled operatives in Faerûn: trained bards, paladins, warriors, and mages who move through the world eliminating threats before the common folk even know they exist.

In 1508 DR, the Alliance is under serious pressure. The Spree Alliance has been attacking its cities systematically — Yartar was assaulted, Silverymoon's wards were destroyed, and the Alliance's most celebrated champion (Mourn Eveningfall) was gravely wounded in battle. The city of Silverymoon — long the crown jewel of the Alliance in the North — is now under Fey Alliance control.

The Alliance continues to function. Waterdeep remains strong. Agents remain active. But the direction is downward, and everyone who pays attention knows it.

## What They Want
- Keep the cities of the North safe and prosperous
- Maintain a united front against chaos and dark powers
- Eliminate threats proactively — before they reach the people
- Preserve the established order of civilization and the gods

## Agent Ranks
| Rank | Title |
|------|-------|
| 1 | Cloak |
| 2 | Redknife |
| 3 | Stingblade |
| 4 | Warduke |
| 5 | Lioncrown |

## Notable Figures
- **Laeral Silverhand** — Open Lord of Waterdeep; one of the most powerful figures in the North
- **Lady Alustriel Silverhand** — Former High Lady of Silverymoon; Chosen of Mystra
- **Mourn Eveningfall** — Named Knight of ALL Realms; gravely wounded

## How They View the Spree
The Alliance sees the Spree as a dangerous extremist organization — heretics who reject the gods and use that ideology as cover for something darker. They consider the Spree an existential threat to the stability of the North and have been fighting them openly and through agents for years. So far, the Alliance has been losing ground.

## Their View of the Party
TBD — depending on what relationship the party has established with Alliance representatives.

name: The Order of the Gauntlet
headquarters: No fixed seat — operates across many temples and fortresses
body:
## What You Know

The Order of the Gauntlet is a relatively new organization dedicated to one thing: finding evil and destroying it. Not theorizing about it. Not monitoring it from a safe distance. Smashing it.

The Order's members — paladins, monks, clerics — understand that evil doesn't announce itself. It wears disguises. It hides inside institutions, inside minds, inside the spaces between laws. The Order is trained to see through the disguise and act. They don't wait for an evil deed to become an atrocity before responding.

What separates the Order from simple zealots is an internal discipline they take as seriously as any external threat. Every member spends long hours in prayer and self-examination, convinced that the greatest vulnerability to evil is the darkness within yourself. They believe you can't fight the dragon in the world until you've slain the dragon within. That conviction gives them a clarity that other martial orders sometimes lack.

The Order doesn't try to rule. They don't want to control minds or build empires. They want to destroy what is evil, inspire what is good, and trust that people will make better choices when darkness stops having a foothold.

## What They Want
- Identify and eliminate evil threats before they can spread
- Enforce justice — punish evil deeds, never evil thoughts
- Be armed, vigilant, and always ready
- Hold to faith: in their god, their companions, and themselves

## Member Ranks
| Rank | Title |
|------|-------|
| 1 | Chevall |
| 2 | Marcheon |
| 3 | Whitehawk |
| 4 | Vindicator |
| 5 | Righteous Hand |

## How They View the Spree
The Order sees the Spree as a heretical organization threatening the spiritual balance of Faerûn. The Spree's rejection of the gods and fixation on Shadow strikes them as dangerous evil masquerading as ideology. They would consider active opposition to the Spree a righteous duty.

## Their View of the Party
TBD — depending on what relationship the party has established with Order members.

name: The Purple Dragons
body:
## What You Know

The Purple Dragons are Cormyr's legendary military — the standing army and elite soldiers who have kept the Forest Kingdom standing through centuries of war, invasion, and political upheaval. They are professional, disciplined, and fiercely loyal to the Obarskyr crown.

Cormyr has survived threats that would have shattered most nations: the Goblin War, the death of King Azoun IV, Shadovar attacks, Zhentarim infiltration, and the fall of Myth Drannor. In each case, the Purple Dragons were at the center of the defense. They don't retreat. They don't negotiate with existential threats. They fight.

Alongside the War Wizards — Cormyr's arcane intelligence and combat corps — the Purple Dragons represent one of the most capable combined-arms forces in Faerûn. Queen Raedra Obarskyr relies on them heavily as she works to rebuild the nation and hold its borders against ongoing threats.

## What They Want
- Protect Cormyr and the Obarskyr crown at all costs
- Maintain order and security throughout the Forest Kingdom
- Defend Cormyr's borders and allies
- Uphold the law and the honor of the Purple Dragon

## Their View of the Party
TBD — depending on what relationship the party has established with Cormyrean forces.

name: The Spree Alliance
insignia: A caduceus with spiked snakes and dragon wings
body:
## What You Know

The Spree Alliance is the primary threat to the North in 1508 DR. They are a shadow organization with a coherent ideology: they believe the gods are a cage — that divine power holds mortals back from their true potential — and they want to tear it all down. They are obsessed with death, the Shadowfell, and the power of dragons. They believe the Shadow Weave is superior to the Weave, and that Shar is the true patron of magic.

On the surface, Spree propaganda presents this as a liberation movement — freedom from divine oppression, self-governance for mortals. Underneath that, they are a cult infrastructure. They gather intelligence for powerful undead masters, fund lich transformations, and guard phylacteries. Their most devoted members welcome death as a transition into Shadow, not an ending.

The Spree has proven capable of military force. They assaulted Yartar — the party helped repel them. They invaded Silverymoon — and while their army was stopped, the city's magical wards were destroyed in the battle. Silverymoon fell into Fey Alliance occupation. That counts as a Spree strategic win.

## What They Want
- Eliminate the gods and dismantle divine infrastructure
- Replace the old order with self-governance free from divine mandate
- Support the rise of dreadlords — the powerful undead figures at the top of their hierarchy
- Spread their ideology through propaganda, infiltration, and force

## Known Members
- **Valas Halfinas** — The figure believed to lead the Spree (known to the party as a primary villain)
- **Bryleth** — Tiefling druid; active in the High Forest region; a significant threat in Chapter 4

## Internal Structure
The Spree has a four-tier hierarchy:

| Tier | Title | Role |
|------|-------|------|
| 1 | Shadow Council | Strategy and leadership |
| 2 | Dreadlord's Hand | Regional commanders |
| 3 | Shadowblade | Local operations, recruitment |
| 4 | Shadowborn | Foot soldiers |

## How They View Other Factions
- **Lord's Alliance** — Primary target; the obstacle to dismantling divine infrastructure
- **Fey Alliance** — Not directly at war, but the Fey see the Spree as a deep threat to the natural order
- **The party** — A persistent interference that has already cost them Yartar

## What You Don't Know
There are things about the Spree that remain unclear — who truly leads them, what their full agenda is, and who else might be pulling strings above even the Shadow Council.

name: The Dead Rabbits
body:
## What You Know

More to come as the party learns more about this faction.

name: The Zhentarim
headquarters: Zhentil Keep (ruined); networks in most major cities
insignia: Gold coin stamped with the Black Network symbol
body:
## What You Know

The Zhentarim — the Black Network — are mercenaries, rogues, and warlocks who want one thing above all: power. Not the abstract kind. Real, tangible, actionable power: wealth, influence, access, and the ability to make things happen when other organizations can't or won't.

They're not strictly villains, though they're not strictly lawful either. They walk a very fine line on legality and don't shy away from shady deals when those deals get results. To the Zhentarim, wealth is power, and power is the point. Their agents are known for being well-equipped, well-paid, and efficient. When a merchant needs reliable caravan guards, when a noble needs bodyguards who won't ask questions, when a city needs soldiers who will fight without requiring months of convincing — the Black Network delivers.

The organization rewards individual ambition. Members are expected to seize opportunities, show initiative, and grow their own influence within the Network. The Zhentarim doesn't hold your hand. It opens doors and expects you to walk through them.

## What They Want
- Accumulate wealth
- Gain influence over powerful individuals and organizations
- Seize opportunities for power wherever they appear
- Ultimately: dominate the flow of commerce and mercenary work across Faerûn

## Member Ranks
| Rank | Title |
|------|-------|
| 1 | Fang |
| 2 | Wolf |
| 3 | Viper |
| 4 | Ardragon |
| 5 | Dread Lord |

## How They View the Spree
The Zhentarim see the Spree as a rival organization with similar methods but more extreme ideology. They would be suspicious of Spree operations that threaten their own influence, but might not oppose them openly unless the Spree directly interfered with Zhentarim interests.

## Their View of the Party
TBD — depending on what relationship the party has established with Black Network agents.
